exportStrategy.serviceType: Export strategy and independent multi-market audit: brand, positioning, pricing, distribution, fulfilment, and channels

about.title: About: A Current Practitioner, Still in the Market
about.description: A bilingual commercial leader active in premium wine since 2008, advising a limited number of brands alongside a senior commercial role. US market access, distribution, pricing, and cross-cultural EN/FR work.

contact.title: Request an Introduction
contact.description: Tell me about your brand. I take on a limited number of engagements at a time and will come back to you if it's a fit.

common.selective: I take on a limited number of engagements at a time.

hero.title: The US is my specialism, but it isn't the only way I can help.
hero.lede: I help premium wine and spirits brands enter and grow in the United States — pricing, distribution, and the order you do things in. The same hands-on judgement carries into export strategy further afield, ongoing advisory, and speaking.

positioning.title: A practitioner, still active in the market.
positioning.body: I hold a senior commercial role in the premium wine industry and advise a limited number of brands alongside it. That means current market knowledge: what importers are actually buying, what distributors are dropping, and where the shelf and the by-the-glass list are moving. Not a playbook from a few years ago, but what's happening right now.

pillars.title: Where brands lose time and margin

pricing.title: Pricing architecture
pricing.body: The number on the shelf is decided three margins earlier. Get the ladder wrong, and even great selling struggles to recover it.

distribution.title: Distribution fit
distribution.body: The right importer for a 2,000-case brand is the wrong one for a 200-case brand. It's an easy introduction to get backwards.

sequencing.title: Market sequencing
sequencing.body: Which markets, which channels, in which order. The wrong sequence burns capital and goodwill that's hard to win back.

results.title: A few things I've helped make happen.

items.0: Opened a major US state market from zero. Within a year, it was one of the brand's three largest US territories.
items.1: Helped a premium winery roughly double its US revenue in a single year.
items.2: Grew one national retail-chain account around sevenfold year on year.
items.3: Lifted a European wine group's turnover 1.9× and its gross margin by eleven points.
items.4: Took a struggling Paris wine shop from 9% to 34% gross margin, then sold the business.

results.note: Brands and clients kept anonymous out of respect for their confidentiality.

diagnosticTeaser.title: An honest read, before anyone signs anything
diagnosticTeaser.body: Tell me about your brand and where you want to take it. We'll start with where you actually stand — in the US, France, the UK, or further afield — and the questions worth answering before you commit budget.

selectiveBlock.title: Deliberately small
selectiveBlock.body: I take on a limited number of engagements at a time, because the work is hands-on and because I'm doing it alongside a full-time commercial role. If the timing isn't right, I'll say so.

speakingCallout.title: Also available for seminars and speaking.
speakingCallout.body: Adapting the product to the market is one thing; adapting the approach is another. I teach and speak on cross-cultural commercial strategy across the US, France, and the UK, for trade audiences, teams, and universities.

hero.title: From a blank page to a full review of an established export business.
hero.lede: Whether you're planning your first export market or already running across a dozen, this is an independent, hands-on read of where your brand stands and where it's quietly leaking value: brand, pricing, partners, fulfilment, and the cultural fit that decides it all.

scratch.title: Starting from scratch
scratch.body: No export presence yet, or a first market in your sights. We build the strategy from the ground up: where to go, in what order, at what price, and through whom.

established.title: An established business
established.body: Already selling across several markets. We audit what you've built, market by market, and name what's working, what's costing you, and what to fix first.

brand.title: Brand & positioning
brand.body: Whether the brand reads the way you intend in each market, and where the story is getting lost between the cellar and the shelf.

culture.title: Cultural fit per market
culture.body: How the brand, the pitch, and the commercial approach land in each culture you sell into. The same wine earns trust differently in New York, London, and Dubai.

pricing.title: Pricing & margin integrity
pricing.body: The full price walk in each market, where margin is leaking between your gate and the consumer, and whether your ladder still holds.

distribution.title: Distribution & partners
distribution.body: An honest assessment of the importers, distributors, and agents you rely on: who's performing, who's coasting, and where a relationship has quietly stopped working.

fulfilment.title: Fulfilment & supply chain
fulfilment.body: Freight, logistics, lead times, and landed cost across your routes: where the chain is fragile, slow, or more expensive than it needs to be.

channel.title: Channel performance
channel.body: On- and off-premise, chains and independents, e-commerce: which channels are genuinely carrying the brand in each market, and which are flattering the numbers.

markets.title: Where I work
markets.body: The sharpest focus is the United States, France, and the United Kingdom, where I work most closely. But export rarely stops there: I've worked across Latin America and the Middle East too, and I'm glad to talk through any market you're weighing. If I'm not the right person for a given market, I'll tell you.

one.title: We scope it together
one.body: A first conversation to map your markets, your ambitions, and where you suspect the value is leaking. We agree what we'll cover.

two.title: I build or review
two.body: Either an export strategy from the ground up, or an independent review across each market you sell into, against current trade conditions, not a playbook.

three.title: You get it in writing
three.body: A clear document: the plan, or the findings, with a prioritised list of what to do first, ranked by impact and yours to act on.

four.title: We debrief
four.body: A working session to walk through it together and agree the order of play. Where it points to deeper work, we'll talk about that separately.

cta.title: Start the conversation
cta.body: Tell me about your brand and the markets you're weighing or already in. If it's a fit, I'll come back to you and we'll take it from there.

hero.title: Find the right distributors and resellers, market by market.
hero.lede: A tool for wineries that takes the guesswork out of who to approach. It surfaces and prioritises the distributors and resellers worth your time in each market, so your outreach starts with a list that's actually worth working.

what.title: What it does
what.body: Most producers spend their best outreach on the wrong doors. The finder builds a focused, prioritised shortlist of distributors and resellers in a market, so you put your energy into the partners most likely to carry your brand.

0.title: A real shortlist
0.body: Not a directory dump. A focused set of distributors and resellers that fit your category, price, and volume.

1.title: Prioritised by fit
1.body: Ranked so you start with the partners most likely to say yes, and most worth your time.

2.title: Market by market
2.body: Run it for each market you're targeting, so your approach is tailored rather than scattered.

3.title: Built for wineries
3.body: Designed around how wine actually reaches shelves and lists, not a generic B2B lead tool.

4.title: Export to Sheets or CSV
4.body: Every list and result is yours to take with you. Export straight to Google Sheets or download as a .csv.

5.title: Your branding, optional
5.body: Make the experience your own. The interface can be customised to your internal branding so it feels in-house.

individual.title: Individual licence
individual.body: For a single user running their own markets: everything the finder does, on one seat.

team.title: Team licence
team.body: For commercial teams working markets together: shared access and shared lists across multiple seats.

platforms.title: Available everywhere you work
platforms.body: Use it in any browser, or on the move with the iOS and Android apps. Your lists stay in sync.

cta.title: Try the distributor & reseller finder
cta.body: Open the tool to see how it builds your shortlist. Questions about whether it fits your brand? Get in touch.

hero.title: Commercial strategy for brands that are past the guesswork.
hero.lede: Ongoing advisory for premium wine and spirits brands building real presence in the US and selected international markets: distribution, pricing, channel, and the commercial discipline to make them hold together.

marketEntry.title: US market access
marketEntry.body: Entry strategy and execution: the realistic path from where you are to shelves and lists that actually move volume.

distribution.title: Importer & distributor strategy
distribution.body: Choosing, winning, and managing the right distribution partners, and knowing when a relationship has stopped working.

pricing.title: Pricing architecture
pricing.body: Building the price ladder backwards from the shelf so margin survives every hand it passes through.

channel.title: Channel & account targeting
channel.body: On- and off-premise, chains and independents, e-commerce: which channels, which accounts, and in what order.

data.title: Commercial data analysis
data.body: Depletion trends, account performance, and the numbers that tell you where to push and where to stop.

positioning.title: Premium positioning
positioning.body: Holding a brand's premium position through the commercial decisions that usually erode it.

markets.title: Where I work
markets.body: Primary focus on the United States, with bilingual EN/FR work across the UK, the UAE, and Northern Europe. The cross-cultural piece is not a footnote. Most commercial relationships in this industry fail at the cultural seam, not on the spreadsheet.

cta.title: Start with an export review
cta.body: Advisory engagements usually begin with an export strategy or audit. It's how we both find out whether there's work worth doing together.

hero.title: Available for seminars, keynotes, and team education.
hero.lede: I take on one-off speaking engagements and educational sessions alongside my advisory work, on the commercial side of wine and spirits, and on the cultural fluency the trade runs on. In English or French.

education.title: Adapting the product to the market is one thing. Adapting the approach is another.
education.body: A wine that sells itself in Paris can stall in New York for reasons that have nothing to do with the wine. How a deal is opened, how a relationship is built, how a price is discussed, what a buyer expects left unsaid: these differ across the US, France, the UK, and beyond, and they decide outcomes more often than the liquid in the bottle. I help teams and audiences read those differences and work with them rather than against them.

0.title: Market culture
0.body: How the US, French, and British trades actually behave, and what's normal in one room but a misstep in the next.

1.title: Negotiation & relationship norms
1.body: Where directness reads as confidence and where it reads as rudeness, and how trust is built in each market.

2.title: Trade expectations
2.body: What importers, distributors, and buyers assume you already know: the unwritten rules that gate access.

3.title: Language & register
3.body: Working credibly in English and French, and knowing which register a given room expects.

formats.title: How I can help
formats.body: Available for one-off engagements, and I'll tailor each session to your audience.

0.title: Keynotes & talks
0.body: Trade events, conferences, and consumer-facing evenings.

1.title: Seminars & workshops
1.body: In-house sessions for sales teams, importers, and producers.

2.title: Panels & moderation
2.body: Contributing to or chairing trade and cultural discussions.

3.title: Tastings with context
3.body: Guided tastings framed around market and cultural strategy.

academic.title: Equally at home in a lecture hall.
academic.body: Before wine, my background was academic, and I remain comfortable in a teaching setting, so I'm also open to university and educational work. I hold an MSc in Language Sciences and Neuroscience from University College London, held a research post at the École Normale Supérieure in Paris, and spent a short period as a PhD candidate. I mention it not as a headline, but so it's clear the door is open if a teaching or lecturing brief would be a fit.

credentials.1: Research post, École Normale Supérieure, Paris

cta.title: Request Daley for an event
cta.body: Tell me about your audience or brief and I'll come back to you. Speaking enquiries are welcome in English or French.

hero.title: A current practitioner, advising a handful of brands alongside the day job.
hero.lede: I hold a senior commercial role in the premium wine industry and take on a limited number of advisory engagements alongside it.

body.p1: I've been in premium wine since 2008, and now lead US commercial efforts for a fine-wine estate: the day-to-day of importer relationships, distributor management, pricing, and the slow work of building real presence in a market that doesn't hand it to you. Before that, I owned and ran two wine retail businesses in Paris.
body.p2: That combination, operator and merchant, US and Europe, is really the point. I've sat on both sides of the table: the brand trying to get in, and the buyer deciding whether to let it. I work in English and French, and most of what I do lives in the gap between how the two commercial cultures actually behave.
body.p3: I advise a small number of brands because the work is hands-on, and because I'm doing it alongside a full-time role. Staying active in the market is the point: what I bring to your brand is current rather than remembered.
body.linkedin: The full professional record is on LinkedIn
body.parisCaption: À l'ombre d'un bouchon — one of my two wine shops in Paris.

cta.title: Request an introduction
cta.body: If you're building or growing a premium wine or spirits brand abroad, start with an export strategy or audit.

hero.title: Tell me about your brand.
hero.lede: Tell me a little about your brand and where you're trying to take it. I only work with a handful of brands at a time, so I read every message myself and I'll come back to you if I think I can genuinely help.

form.currentDistributionPlaceholder: Importer / distributor status, or none yet…
form.freeText: Tell me what's actually going on
form.freeTextPlaceholder: What are you trying to do, and what isn't working? The more specific you are, the more useful the diagnostic.
form.consent: I'd like to receive a written diagnostic by email.
form.pricingSection: Pricing details — optional, but helps us model the numbers
form.wineNamesPlaceholder: e.g. Château Margaux 2021, Chablis Premier Cru…
form.wineStylePlaceholder: e.g. Red Bordeaux blend, Chablis, Single malt…

success.title: Thank you. It's with me.
success.body: I read every message personally. If your brand is a fit for the way I work, I'll come back to you with a diagnostic. If the timing isn't right, I'll tell you that too.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again, or email me directly.
errors.name: Please tell me your name.
errors.email: Please enter a valid email address.
errors.company: Please tell me your brand or company name.
errors.freeText: A sentence or two here makes all the difference.
errors.consent: Please confirm you'd like to receive the diagnostic.
errors.rateLimited: That's a few submissions in quick succession. Please wait a moment and try again.

footer.tagline: Commercial strategy for premium wine & spirits brands. US-focused, bilingual EN/FR.
footer.selective: A limited number of engagements at a time.
